stress.where: The word 'feature' itself: used simultaneously for (a) a direction in activation space, (b) an SAE dictionary latent, (c) a human-interpretable concept, (d) a causal mediator of behavior
stress.why: One term carries four operationalizations that empirically dissociate: SAE latents with high auto-interp scores often fail as causal mediators; probe directions that decode a concept often aren't the directions the model uses. Practitioners patch by qualifying ('functional feature', 'atomic feature', 'true feature') without a definition that predicts when the four coincide.

killed.name: Feature Role Signature (mandatory 4-axis certificate)
killed.why: Feature absorption (Chanin et al. 2024, documented in Gemma-Scope-class SAEs) breaks the ontology from inside: a latent labeled C="token starts with E" certifies as an 'aligned feature' — held-out probe AUC stays high (absorption only dents recall on a handful of tokens), precision@k of top activations is ~1.0 (every top-activating input is an E-word), interchange intervention along v moves first-letter behavior, and simulation scoring correlates well — yet the direction is gerrymandered and silently dead on "elephant" because a co-trained latent absorbed it. The certificate awards the reserved bare noun 'feature' to a non-atomic, absorbed direction, so the aligned region does not carve feature-hood at its joints (same failure for v=(v1+v2)/sqrt(2) of two real features with C="C1 or C2", which also clears all four axes). The certificate is also not even well-formed for arbitrary (v,L,C): med = interchange effect / full-layer patch effect divides by ~0 whenever C is not causally used at layer L for the chosen output metric (e.g., a sentiment latent, middle layer, syntax-continuation task), and int >= 0.8 simulation-Pearson is so rare in practice (Bills et al. 2023: top explained units average ~0.3-0.4) that the 'feature' region is nearly empty — a vocabulary that names almost nothing.

concept.name: Read/Write Feature Split (dual-basis typed features)
concept.definition: Ban the bare term 'feature direction'; every concept-C claim at layer L must name a typed pair (w, r) plus mismatch angle. Write-direction w: unit vector in the span of upstream-component outputs, fit by regressing component outputs against C labels. Read-direction r: argmax over unit vectors of the attribution-weighted alignment sum a_j * cos^2(r, row_j) where row_j ranges over input-weight rows of downstream components and a_j is that component's causal attribution to C-behavior (via path/attribution patching). Mismatch theta = arccos|<w,r>| is a mandatory reported quantity. Probe directions are a third derived object, not a feature.
concept.prediction: Across concepts and layers in one open model (e.g., Gemma-2-2B + Gemma-Scope), the mediation effect of patching along any candidate direction d is predicted by read-alignment cos^2(d, r) with R^2 >= 0.5, while held-out probe AUC of d adds < 0.05 incremental R^2; and projecting a decodable-but-non-mediating probe direction onto the read-cone yields a direction recovering >= 70% of the full-layer-patch behavioral effect. If read-alignment predicts mediation no better than probe accuracy does, the split is dead.
concept.reduces_to: The 'reading vs writing directions' remark in Elhage et al.'s Mathematical Framework, plus the amnesic-probing/LEACE result that decodable != used — possibly just formalizing folklore that already exists.

test.design: Falsification on gpt2-small (CPU; Gemma-2-2B out of budget — honest scope reduction, same logical structure), layers {5,8} resid_pre, last token, 6 word-category concepts, ONE intervention (projection patching along a unit direction d: move base activation's d-component to the source value). Per concept×layer group: read direction r = normalized gradient of the behavioral metric (category-vs-union logit contrast) at the hook — the steelman "what downstream reads"; candidate pool of 12 directions per group (probe diff-in-means p, r, mean-diff Δ-hat, p-orthogonalized-to-r d_nm, p/r and p/random mixes, 3 randoms) → 144 pooled points. Measured: normalized mediation m(d)=E(d)/E_full, read-alignment cos²(d,r), held-out probe AUC of d (different prompt templates). PREREGISTERED (committed 728571b BEFORE running, maevtica repo, experiments/read_write_split/PREREG.md): SUPPORTED iff R²[m~cos²] >= 0.50 AND incremental R² from adding AUC < 0.05 AND median E(r)/E_full recovery >= 0.70; REFUTED iff R²_read <= R²_AUC (concept's own kill clause) OR R²_read < 0.25 OR recovery < 0.35; else INCONCLUSIVE. One prereg amendment after run 1: the O2 code-correctness oracle had to use per-prompt Δ-hat (where patch=full-patch is a true identity), not the mean-diff direction; no decision threshold changed.
test.numbers: n=144 directions across 12 concept×layer groups. R²_read (m ~ cos²(d,r)) = 0.100 — far below the predicted >= 0.5. R²_AUC alone = 0.052; incremental R² of AUC over cos² = 0.020 (that sub-clause alone would have passed). Median read-direction recovery E(r)/E_full = 0.170 vs predicted >= 0.70 (per-group range 0.02–0.44). Decodable-but-non-mediating directions DO exist: d_nm qualified (AUC>=0.8, |m|<0.3) in 12/12 groups (mean AUC(d_nm)=0.98, mean m(d_nm)=0.019). Kill clause narrowly not triggered (0.100 > 0.052), but both hard REFUTED thresholds hit (R²_read < 0.25 AND recovery < 0.35). Diagnostic secondary: the bilinear predictor (d·Δ)(d·r) gets R² = 0.629 — mediation needs write-content alignment times read alignment; read-alignment alone is not the predictor the concept claims.
test.sanity: Oracles all passed on the final run: O1 effect exists — mean full-layer-patch effect 4.34 logits (per-group 2.1–9.5, all positive); O2 code correctness — patching along per-prompt Δ-hat must equal the full patch by construction: median |m−1| = 0.0000 (exact); O3 AUC pipeline — held-out AUC(probe) = 0.986, AUC(random) = 0.695 (inside preregistered [0.3,0.7]; inflated by the sign-free max(a,1−a) fold with small n — noted limit). The oracle discipline earned its keep again: run 1 returned BROKEN_MEASUREMENT because O2 was computed from the mean-diff direction (median |m−1| = 0.334) — a broken oracle implementation, caught and fixed without touching any decision threshold; primary numbers were identical across both runs.

concept.name: Behavioral Quotient Feature (intervention-equivalence class)
concept.definition: Fix layer L, input distribution D, and behavioral readout b (e.g., a task logit-diff). Two activation perturbations delta1, delta2 (norm <= rho) are equivalent iff E_D|b(x; h_L+delta1) - b(x; h_L+delta2)| < epsilon. A feature is a coordinate of the quotient space (activation space modulo behavior-preserving perturbations), estimated as top singular directions of the D-averaged Jacobian of b w.r.t. h_L. Feature claims are statements about quotient coordinates; SAE latents and probe directions get classified by which quotient coordinate(s) their vectors project onto. 'Interpretable concept' and 'causal mediator' coincide by construction iff they land on the same coordinate.
concept.prediction: Two computable clauses: (i) the effective rank k(L) (singular directions covering 90% of behavioral variance) stays within 10% when SAE dictionary width grows 4x, while latent count grows 4x — quotient dimension is a property of the model, not the dictionary; (ii) any two SAE latents whose decoder vectors project mainly onto the same quotient coordinate produce steering effects with logit-diff-vector cosine > 0.8 even when their decoder cosine is < 0.1. Falsified if same-coordinate latents steer incoherently or if effective rank tracks dictionary size.
concept.reduces_to: Causal abstraction / Distributed Alignment Search (Geiger et al.) — risk that this is DAS restated as a quotient construction, i.e., 'the aligned subspace' under a new name.

test.design: Preregistered in /home/friemann/workspace/repos-demidovz/maevtica/research_cycle/experiments/quotient_bq/PREREG.md BEFORE running (decision rule fixed there; code exp.py, output result.json + effects.npz). Setup: gpt2-small layer 6 resid_post, OpenAI v5 TopK SAEs at widths 32768 and 131072 (exactly 4x, same recipe/location — the only ready-made 4x pair on a CPU-sized model). Steer with alpha*unit(decoder_i) (alpha=0.5*mean resid norm=45.6) at all positions; effect vector = mean over 8 fixed probes of last-token logit diff; N=256 alive latents sampled per SAE, seed 0. Clause (i) rule: k90 (singular dirs covering 90% of centered effect variance) — supported if |Δ|/min ≤ 0.10, refuted if ratio ≥ 1.5, inconclusive if sample-limited (k90 ≥ 0.8N). Clause (ii) rule: quotient coords = top-k90 left singular vectors of ridge-fit map from 32k decoders to 32k effects (fit decoupled from 128k test set); qualifying 128k pairs = both dominance ≥0.5 (ladder to 0.4 if <5 pairs), same argmax coordinate, decoder |cos|<0.1; supported if median sign-corrected effect cos > 0.8 AND ≥0.2 above a fair null (random low-decoder-cos pairs), refuted if < 0.4. Overall: SUPPORTED iff both; REFUTED iff either refuted.
test.numbers: Clause (i): k90_32k=96, k90_128k=93 — 3.2% difference (bound 10%) while dictionary grew 4x → supported-i, and NOT sample-limited (96,93 << 0.8*256). Key diagnostic: decoder-geometry k90 = 148/143, i.e. behavioral rank (~95) sits clearly BELOW dictionary geometry rank — the rank is behavior-driven, not decoder-span-driven. Clause (ii): the premise population is EMPTY — 0 of 256 sampled 128k latents put ≥40% of quotient-projection energy on any single coordinate (K=96), so 0 qualifying pairs even after the preregistered 0.4 fallback → inconclusive_no_pairs (clause vacuous as operationalized). Fair null for pair cosines: 0.129. Exploratory (post-hoc, NOT decisive): over 500 low-decoder-cos 128k pairs, spearman(quotient-profile similarity, effect cosine) = 0.52 (graded quotient structure is real), but the 3 pairs with profile-sim > 0.8 had median effect cos 0.32 — far below the predicted 0.8, leaning AGAINST the strong coherence threshold.
test.sanity: All oracles passed, measurement valid: O1 split-half reliability (probes 0-3 vs 4-7) median cos = 0.828 across all 512 steered latents (threshold 0.6); O2 positive control (rescale 1.1*alpha, preregistered fallback since only 2 cross-SAE near-duplicate pairs existed) median effect cos = 0.999 (threshold 0.9); O3 mean effect norm 192-195 vs repeat-clean noise floor 0.0. No BROKEN_MEASUREMENT.
